solution.p: Sometimes we need to square both sides of an equation twice.

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned} {\sqrt{t+8}} \amp = {\sqrt{t}+2} \\ ({\sqrt{t+8}})^2 \amp = ({\sqrt{t}+2})^2 \\ t+8 \amp = (\sqrt{t})^2-2\cdot-2\sqrt{t}+(-2)^2 \\ t+8 \amp = t + 4\sqrt{t}+4 \\ t+8 \mathbf{{}-t} \amp = t + 4\sqrt{t}+4 \mathbf{{}-t} \\ 8 \amp = + 4\sqrt{t}+4 \\ 4 \amp = + 4\sqrt{t} \\ \frac{4}{ + 4} \amp = \frac{ + 4\sqrt{t}}{ + 4} \\ 1 \amp = \sqrt{t} \\ (1)^2 \amp = (\sqrt{t})^2 \\ 1 \amp = t \end{aligned} }

solution.p: Whenever we square both sides of an equation, we could create extraneous solutions. We must check our solution.
We will substitute t=1 into {\sqrt{t+8}}={\sqrt{t}+2}, and we have:
We checked that t=1 is a solution.
